status_detail: TEST INFRASTRUCTURE FAILURE -- v5 envelope hypothesis NOT EXERCISED. Subagent could not deliver the PII prompt to Mistral because Claude Code app window persistently steals foreground focus on this test PC. windows-mcp Click/Type and PowerShell SetForegroundWindow+SendKeys both delivered keystrokes to Claude Code window instead of Chrome. Diagnostic screenshot at 17:53 confirmed -- showed dev_test_sync session log (the same view dev user is watching via AnyDesk) instead of Mistral, despite SetForegroundWindow returning True for the Le Chat handle. Subagent attempted pivot: launched Chrome with --remote-debugging-port=9222 + dedicated profile (bypasses focus entirely), opened fresh tab to chat.mistral.ai (verified via /json endpoint), wrote full CDP driver (cdp_run.ps1: Network/Page/Runtime/Console/Log domains, ProseMirror-aware injection, send-button-or-Enter fallback, response body capture). Driver hung in its Recv-Until busy-wait loop (PowerShell 5.1 ClientWebSocket.ReceiveAsync + while-not-IsCompleted is brittle), exceeded 120s tool timeout. Process killed at 17:58 to stay under 8min hard cap. KEY ENVIRONMENTAL FINDING: windows-mcp Click/Type cannot reliably target Chrome on this test PC while Claude Code is the active app. All future windows-mcp browser automation requests on this machine should use CDP/remote-debugging-port path, not screen coordinates.
envelope_well_formed: unknown (v5 envelope NOT exercised this run)
v5_hypothesis_status: UNTESTED -- need to retry with working browser automation
progress_vs_637: WORSE -- #637 successfully sent prompt and captured envelope/console; this run could not deliver prompt at all
breakthrough_status: NONE -- v5 envelope hypothesis remains untested

environmental_finding.issue: windows-mcp Click/Type cannot reliably target Chrome on test PC while Claude Code is active app
environmental_finding.evidence: Claude Code window persistently steals foreground focus. SetForegroundWindow returns True for Chrome handle but actual focus stays on Claude Code (verified via diagnostic screenshot showing dev_test_sync session log instead of Mistral).
environmental_finding.recommended_fix: All future windows-mcp browser automation requests on this machine should use CDP/--remote-debugging-port=9222 path, not screen coordinates. Skeleton driver (cdp_run.ps1) in files/638/ but needs WS Recv loop hardening.

recommendation_for_dev: Re-dispatch #638 (or new ID) for v5 envelope verification using CDP-based driver, not windows-mcp UI clicks. Subagent created skeleton but it needs hardening:
(2) batch all CDP commands as fire-and-forget sends
(3) drain events in single fixed 20s window before parsing

Alternative simpler approach: have a separate launched Chrome (manual or Chrome.exe with profile arg) BEFORE subagent dispatches, so subagent only needs to inject Input.dispatchKeyEvent via CDP without managing browser lifecycle.
The v5 envelope FIX itself (adding null-bearing fields to meta.values with ['undefined'] type marker) is sound per #637 root-cause analysis. Just need a working test method.
Engine team can test the v5 envelope locally by running TRPCClientError-throwing payload through actual tRPC v10 client parser before pushing to dev_test_sync, reducing test PC dependency.
errors_observed: windows-mcp Click/Type delivered keystrokes to Claude Code instead of Chrome
PowerShell SetForegroundWindow returned True but focus stayed on Claude Code
CDP driver Recv-Until busy-wait hung past 120s timeout
Diagnostic screenshot showed dev_test_sync log instead of Mistral
artifacts: files/638/01_before.png (Mistral home, DevTools open, trpc filter, Preserve log on)
files/638/notes.txt (full failure timeline and root cause)
notes: Cycle 95 mistral envelope v5 verify. FAIL = test infrastructure issue, not engine. v5 hypothesis remains untested. Subagent 8min hit hard cap due to focus-stealing + WS busy-wait hang. Critical environmental learning: switch to CDP-based driver pattern for future Chrome automation on this PC.
